summary: This lesson covers demonstrative pronouns/adjectives (este/esta/esto, ese/esa/eso and their plurals), the neuter forms esto/eso for non-specific referents, introduces 'tan' (so), 'cuánto/cuántas' (how much/how many), and practices combining tenses in complex sentences.

teachings.summary: Full paradigm of this/that/these/those: este/esta (this), ese/esa (that), estos/estas (these), esos/esas (those). Derived from 'esta noche' as a hook. The neuter forms esto/eso are used for general/non-specific references (not tied to a specific noun).
teachings.rule: Masculine: este/ese (singular), estos/esos (plural). Feminine: esta/esa (singular), estas/esas (plural). Neuter (general, no specific noun): esto/eso. To get 'that' from 'this', remove the t: esta -> esa, este -> ese.

examples.spanish: Nada me gustó más que eso.
examples.english: Nothing pleased me more than that.

teachings.notes: Spanish also allows post-positioned demonstratives: 'el hombre este', 'el hombre ese' -- possibly a structure influenced by Arabic during 800 years of contact.

teachings.summary: Tan means 'so' as in 'so tired' (tan cansado). Used in structures like 'tan... que...' (so... that...).

examples.spanish: Estábamos tan cansados que dormimos todo el día.
examples.english: We were so tired that we slept all day.

examples.spanish: Estábamos tan cansados cuando llegamos que dormimos todo el día.
examples.english: We were so tired when we arrived that we slept all day.

teachings.summary: Cuánto means 'how much'. It agrees in gender and number with what it modifies: cuántas veces (how many times, feminine because vez is feminine).
teachings.rule: Cuánto/cuánta/cuántos/cuántas -- agrees with the noun it modifies.

examples.spanish: No sé cuántas veces lo visité.
examples.english: I don't know how many times I visited him.

examples.spanish: No sé cuántas veces lo visitaron.
examples.english: I don't know how many times they visited him.

examples.spanish: No sé cuántas veces la visitaron.
examples.english: I don't know how many times they visited her.

teachings.notes: To determine gender of 'vez', look back to where it was used: 'otra vez' (not otro vez) confirms it is feminine.

vocabulary.derivation: Literally 'all the day'; todo (not toda) because día is masculine
Related to cuándo (when) -- cuándo with a t
